name: Tennis Ball Demo
source: Chris Atkinson · Y3–Y4 long jump session card

ks1.objective: Children grasp the day's theme via a vivid physical metaphor.
successCriteria: Can repeat one of the three theme words

lks2.objective: Children grasp the day's theme via a vivid physical metaphor — quick push = high bounce.
successCriteria: Can repeat the three theme words (TALL · PUSH · LAND)
Can explain in their own words why a quick push lifts them higher

uks2.objective: Children grasp the day's theme via a vivid physical metaphor and connect it to ground-reaction-force concept.
successCriteria: Can explain the demo using both imagery and 'the ground pushes back'
Can repeat the three theme words and link each to a body action

space.description: Children gathered in a semicircle around the coach. No equipment movement during demo. Indoor or outdoor — needs a hard surface so the tennis ball bounces cleanly off the football.

steps: Gather the children in a tight semicircle so everyone can see the coach's hands.
Show them the tennis ball and the football. "Watch carefully — don't miss it."
Hold the football still at chest height with both hands. Drop the tennis ball directly on top of it.
The tennis ball will ping high. PAUSE. Don't speak — let the moment land.
Reveal: "The tennis ball is you. The football is the ground. When you push quick and strong into the ground, the ground pushes you straight back up — high."
Reveal the three theme words: "TALL. PUSH. LAND." Have them repeat each one.
Close: "Today your job is to be the tennis ball — tall body, quick punch, off you go. Let's go."

cues.trigger: as a recall cue throughout the rest of the session
as the theme reveal at the end

errors.issue: Demo flops — football moves or tennis ball mis-bounces
errors.cause: Football not held firmly, or tennis ball dropped off-centre
errors.fix: Both hands on the football, locked at chest height. Practice drop once before the children arrive — get the contact point right.
DO first, talk second. The pause is the lesson. Don't fill the silence.
Strict 2-min cap. The imagery does the teaching, not the explanation. Move on.

progressions: Standard: drop the tennis ball cleanly, reveal theme, move on.
Comparison version: drop a second time with a TAP (not a release) — lower bounce. "Slow push = low bounce. Quick push = high bounce."
Child-led version: have a confident child do the dropping next time, you narrate.
Recall through the session: any time intensity drops, call "REMEMBER THE TENNIS BALL!"
questions: What happens when you push the ground QUICKLY?
How is your body like the tennis ball?
What does TALL mean for our body?
base.showmanship: Pause for impact after the bounce. Do not fill the silence. Let them be visibly amazed. The imagery does the teaching, not the explanation — your job is to land it and move on.
